commit.message:
fix: add window.__CSRF__ pattern for updated Passport HTML (#9)

* fix: add window.__CSRF__ pattern for updated Passport HTML

Yandex Passport now renders the CSRF token as:

    window.__CSRF__ = "value";

instead of the previous `csrf_token` input/JS/JSON formats. The existing
three patterns all match empty strings on the new page, causing
`CsrfExtractionError`.

Add a fourth pattern to `CSRF_PATTERNS` that matches the new
`window.__CSRF__` global assignment. Existing patterns are preserved for
backward compatibility.

- Add `re.compile(r'window\.__CSRF__\s*=\s*\"([^\"]+)\"\')`
  to `CSRF_PATTERNS` in `constants.py`
- Add `tests/fixtures/csrf_window_global.html` fixture
- Extend `test_csrf_patterns` parametrize to cover the new pattern

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Simplify CSRF regex quoting (use single-quoted raw string)

commit.message:
chore(ci): remove unused force parameter from pack_push workflow

The force flag had no practical use case: for under-review packages
choco.org accepts the push directly (exit 0), and for already-approved
packages overwriting is not possible regardless. Simplify by always
treating 'already exists' as a warning.

GitHub Packages already uses GITHUB_TOKEN which is short-lived and
repo-scoped - equivalent in security to OIDC. No change needed there.

fix(TOP/tb): resolve 4 directed bench Xcelium failures

All 4 are TB issues, NOT RTL bugs — core TDC functionality is intact.

1. stress_position: move pkt_words/pkt_word_count declarations above
   check_word task (Xcelium enforces IEEE 1800 forward-ref scoping)

2. stress_stop_qualifier: change always_ff to always for stop_edge_count
   monitor (initial block also drives it between test phases → MULAXX)

3. tdc_arbiter3_unit: auto-detect mptdc_tb_pkg import in run_tb.sh and
   add MPTDC/tb/common/mptdc_tb_pkg.sv to compile (is_header/is_eoc
   helpers live there, not in the RTL filelists)

4. tdc_shared_readout_unit: add #1 delay before rst_n deassertion to
   avoid scheduling race — when rst_n=1 coincides with posedge clk_sys,
   the idx0 counter's always_ff may execute the reset branch on the
   same cycle the DUT consumes the first META record, missing 1
   handshake count (idx0 ends at 1 instead of 2)

commit.message:
fix(security): integrate Key Vault for secret management (#92)

* fix(security): add Content-Security-Policy and security headers (fixes #86)

Add a restrictive Content-Security-Policy header to staticwebapp.config.json:
- default-src 'self' — only same-origin by default
- script-src 'self' — no inline scripts
- style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' — needed for Fluent UI runtime styles
- img-src 'self' data: https: — allow data URIs and HTTPS images
- connect-src 'self' https://*.openai.azure.com — allow Azure OpenAI API calls
- font-src 'self' data: — allow embedded fonts
- object-src 'none' — block plugins
- frame-ancestors 'none' — prevent framing (clickjacking)
- base-uri 'self' — prevent base tag hijacking
- form-action 'self' — restrict form submissions

X-Content-Type-Options and X-Frame-Options were already present.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(security): add Content-Security-Policy and security headers (fixes #86) (#91)

Add a restrictive Content-Security-Policy header to staticwebapp.config.json:
- default-src 'self' — only same-origin by default
- script-src 'self' — no inline scripts
- style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' — needed for Fluent UI runtime styles
- img-src 'self' data: https: — allow data URIs and HTTPS images
- connect-src 'self' https://*.openai.azure.com — allow Azure OpenAI API calls
- font-src 'self' data: — allow embedded fonts
- object-src 'none' — block plugins
- frame-ancestors 'none' — prevent framing (clickjacking)
- base-uri 'self' — prevent base tag hijacking
- form-action 'self' — restrict form submissions

X-Content-Type-Options and X-Frame-Options were already present.

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(SM-16): restructure resolveUncached — replace lookupFuzzy with targeted index lookups

- Remove single lookupFuzzy call that fed all Tier 2a/2b/3 in resolveUncached
- Tier 2a: iterate importedFiles with lookupExactAll per file (O(imports) × O(1))
- Tier 2b: iterate symbols.getFiles() filtered by isFileInPackageDir + lookupExactAll
  (O(files) × O(1), avoids global name scan)
- Tier 3: replace with lookupClassByName + lookupImplByName + lookupFuzzyCallable
  (three O(1) index lookups covering class-like, Rust impl blocks, and callables)
- Add getFiles() to SymbolTable interface (exposes fileIndex.keys() for Tier 2b)
- Add lookupImplByName() to SymbolTable — dedicated Rust Impl index kept separate
  from classByName to preserve correct heritage-map resolution
- Remove allDefs parameter from walkBindingChain; always use lookupExactAll directly
- Add 29 new unit tests covering SM-16 changes and per-language fixtures
- fuzzyCallCount in getStats() is now 0 for all resolve() calls (acceptance criterion)

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(SM-16): address all PR #764 review findings

1. Eager callableIndex — maintained on add() like classByName/implByName,
   removing the O(globalIndex) lazy rebuild on the Tier 3 hot path.

2. Tier 2b inverted index — packageDirSuffix→Set<filePath> built lazily on
   first Tier 2b hit. Changes O(allFiles×packages) per resolution to
   O(packages×filesInPackage).

3. Tier 3 type exclusion documented — TypeAlias, Const, Variable are
   intentionally not reachable at Tier 3. 4 negative/positive tests added.

4. Tier 3 allocation guard simplified — single spread replaces 4-way if-else.

5. getFiles() live iterator documented with safety contract.

6. Remaining lookupFuzzy callers in call-processor.ts documented in the
   Tier 3 comment block.

7. Tier 2b language fixtures — added Rust, Kotlin, PHP tests (3 new).

Also merges origin/main (SM-15 accumulator fixes).

* fix(SM-16): address Codex adversarial review — Tier 2b cache lifecycle + Macro/Delegate at Tier 3

1. Tier 2b packageDirIndex now invalidated in clearCache() and clear(),
   preventing stale snapshots when symbols/packages are added between
   chunk processing phases.

2. Macro (C/C++) and Delegate (C#) added to CALLABLE_TYPES in the eager
   callableIndex, restoring Tier 3 reachability for these call targets
   that the old lookupFuzzy returned.

* fix(SM-16): address ce:review findings — Tier 2b cache lifecycle + Tier 3 perf + test gaps

1. packageDirIndex no longer invalidated in clearCache() — the index
   persists across file boundaries since packageMap and symbols are
   append-only during the calls phase. Only clear() (pipeline reset)
   invalidates. Prevents O(files×dirs) rebuild per-file.

2. Tier 3 short-circuit: return null before spread when all three
   indexes are empty, avoiding allocation on the common miss path.

3. Add Macro (C/C++) and Delegate (C#) Tier 3 regression tests —
   the only newly-added CALLABLE_TYPES were completely untested.

4. Add packageDirIndex invalidation regression test — verifies clear()
   resets the index and newly-added symbols are visible.

* fix(SM-16): address final review — deduplicate NamedImportMap + doc fixes

1. NamedImportMap: removed duplicate definition from resolution-context.ts,
   now imported directly from import-processor.ts (no re-export needed —
   no consumers imported it from resolution-context).

2. packageDirIndex build cost documented accurately in comment.

3. fuzzyCallCount scope documented in test comment.

4. Tier 2a test suite: added comment about Go/Kotlin/PHP coverage.

commit.message:
Add pvnode als alternative Provider (#256)

* Add pvnode forecast service integration

Add pvnode (pvnode.com) as a fourth forecast provider, based on
the SOLECTRUS forecast-collector implementation. pvnode provides
high-resolution PV forecasts with 15-minute intervals.

Changes:
- New lib/pvnode.js converter module (API response to standard format)
- main.js: pvnode URL building, Bearer auth, response parsing
- admin/jsonConfig.json: pvnode service option, forecast days config
- io-package.json: pvnodeForecastDays default config
- i18n translations for all 11 languages
- Updated German and English documentation

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Update package-lock.json after dependency install

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add pvnode paid/free account handling and extra parameters

- Add pvnodePaid checkbox to distinguish free/paid accounts
  - Free: forecast_days fixed at 1 (today + tomorrow)
  - Paid: forecast_days configurable up to 7
- Add pvnodeExtraParams text field for optional API parameters
  (e.g. diffuse_radiation_model, snow_slide_coefficient, shading_config)
- Forecast days field only visible when paid account is enabled
- Updated all 11 i18n translations and documentation

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add pvnode clearsky, temperature, and weather_code to JSON output

pvnode provides additional data beyond power forecasts. These fields
are now extracted from the API response and included in the per-plant
JSONData and JSONTable output:

- watts_clearsky: clear-sky reference power (W) per timestamp
- temperature: temperature (°C) per timestamp
- weather_code: WMO weather code per timestamp

JSONData entries for pvnode now include: t, y, clearsky, temp, weather_code
JSONTable rows for pvnode now include: Time, Power, Clearsky, Temperature, WeatherCode

Based on SOLECTRUS forecast-collector pvnode_adapter.rb implementation.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add pvnode summary clearsky and request batching with second_array

- Summary JSONData and JSONTable now include summed clearsky values across
  all plants (temperature and weather_code excluded from summary as they
  cannot be meaningfully summed)
- Implement pvnode request batching using second_array API feature: up to
  2 plants per API request, reducing API call count by ~50% (critical for
  free accounts with 40 requests/month limit)
- Extract pvnode fetch logic into dedicated updatePvnodeServiceData method
- Extract shared error handling into handleServiceError method
- Update documentation (EN/DE) with batching and summary notes

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add temperature and weather_code to pvnode summary JSON (first value wins)

Since all plants share the same location, temperature and weather_code
are identical across plants. These values are now included in summary
JSONData and JSONTable using a first-value-wins approach (no addition),
matching the SOLECTRUS accumulate pattern.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add pvnode to README.md service list

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add pvnode configuration image reference to docs

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* fix: correct pvnode summary docs - temperature and weather code are included

The documentation incorrectly stated that temperature and weather code
are only available per plant, not in the summary. In fact, the code
does include them in the summary (using the first plant's values).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add files via upload

* Add files via upload

* Delete docs/en/img/pvforecast-pvnode-options.png

* Add files via upload

* fix: convert pvnode UTC timestamps to local system time

pvnode API returns timestamps in UTC. Without explicit UTC parsing,
moment.js treated them as local time, causing a timezone offset
(e.g. 1 hour behind for CET/UTC+1 users).

Use moment.utc().local() to correctly parse as UTC and convert
to the system's local timezone.

healthcheck: add network-connectivity log check for supervised *arr apps

Prowlarr (and other *arr apps) can lose internet connectivity while the
process continues running, leaving the container incorrectly marked healthy.

Adds two new functions:
- check_app_logs: scans a log file for error patterns within a configurable
  time window (APP_LOG_CHECK_MINUTES, default 5). Uses awk to extract lines
  from the window — including multi-line .NET stack trace continuation lines
  — then greps for each pattern. Returns 1 (unhealthy) on first match.
- check_app_specific: dispatches to check_app_logs for supervised apps
  (jackett, lidarr, prowlarr, radarr, readarr, sonarr) using
  /config/supervisord.log and a set of network-specific patterns.

Pattern rationale:
  SocketException          root .NET transport exception; DNS, connect,
                           EAGAIN, unreachable — almost always network
  Resource temporarily unavailable  EAGAIN (errno 11) — user's exact case
  Network is unreachable   ENETUNREACH (errno 101)
  No route to host         EHOSTUNREACH (errno 113)
  Name or service not known  DNS resolution failure
  Connection timed out     ETIMEDOUT (errno 110)
  HttpClient.Timeout       TaskCanceledException from HTTP client timeout;
                           constrained to avoid matching routine cancellations

Bare HttpRequestException and WebException are intentionally excluded:
they also trigger on 4xx/5xx protocol errors and auth rejections that do
not indicate a network outage.

Key improvements from the alternative approach:

- trySplit() pulls batches into array-backed spliterators that ForkJoinPool can halve

commit.message:
test(serializer): add boundary tests for v19 buffered serializer (#1809)

* test(serializer): add boundary tests for v19 buffered serializer

Add 4 new tests to test_encode_decode.py to improve coverage of the
v19 buffered serializer inline vs standalone blob boundary fix (#1795):

- test_15: Parametric boundary test with node counts 63997-64001
  bracketing the 255991/255992 byte inline/blob threshold
- test_16: Mixed inline + standalone blob labels in a single graph
  (small 100-node label + large 63999-node label)
- test_17: Deleted entity ID lists at boundary (31998*8=255984 inline
  vs 31999*8=255992 standalone blob)
- test_18: Large string properties at boundary (255990 chars inline
  vs 255991 chars standalone blob, accounting for null terminator)

References #1779

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): skip boundary tests under ASAN/Valgrind

The new boundary tests (test_15-18) create hundreds of thousands of
nodes, which exceeds the per-class ASAN timeout (900s). Skip them
under sanitizer/valgrind builds. The existing test_14 already covers
one boundary point and runs under ASAN.

Also removed 63999 from test_15 loop since it's redundant with test_14.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): make tearDown resilient to missing graph key

When tests are skipped under ASAN/Valgrind, tearDown still runs but
the graph key may not exist, causing graph.delete() to throw
'Invalid graph operation on empty key'. Wrap in try/except to prevent
the test process from crashing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(test): rewrite serializer tests per review feedback

Address review from swilly22:
- Remove byte-size boundary assumptions (fragile, depends on GraphBLAS)
- Remove VKEY_MAX_ENTITY_COUNT overrides
- Remove SANITIZER/VALGRIND skip guards (tests are now small enough)
- Narrow tearDown exception to only suppress 'empty key' errors
- 3 focused tests: varied label sizes, deletions across labels, large strings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): also handle connection errors in tearDown

The ASAN sanitizer occasionally kills the Redis server (e.g. timeout),
causing tearDown's graph.delete() to throw ConnectionError. Suppress
both 'empty key' and connection errors since neither is a test failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(test): use parameterized query for large strings

Replace O(n²) REDUCE string concatenation with pre-built Python strings
passed as query parameters. This avoids timeouts under ASAN where the
encode_decode class shares a 900s budget with test_14 (63999 nodes).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
